---
title: "Median Household Income by Neighbourhood"
description: "Census-tract median household income across Canada's cities — 2021 Census"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.config import get_data_date
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_map
```

Median household total income for each **census tract** — roughly a neighbourhood of
2,500–8,000 people — from the 2021 Census. **Zoom and pan to your own area.** Census
tracts cover cities and larger towns; sparsely populated rural areas aren't divided into
tracts, so they appear blank.

This detailed view lives on its own page (≈3 MB of boundary data) so the main
[Income & Inequality](index.qmd) page stays fast to load.

[← Back to Income & Inequality](index.qmd)

```{python}
#| echo: false
geo_path = "data/geo/ct_2021.geojson"
ct_income_path = "data/geo/statcan_ct_income_2021.csv"
if os.path.exists(geo_path) and os.path.exists(ct_income_path):
    ct_geo = json.load(open(geo_path))
    ct_df = pd.read_csv(ct_income_path, dtype={"ctuid": str})
    zlo = float(ct_df["median_income"].quantile(0.05))
    zhi = float(ct_df["median_income"].quantile(0.95))
    fig = choropleth_map(ct_geo, ct_df, "ctuid", "median_income", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="Median<br>household<br>income", value_prefix="$", colorscale="Viridis",
        zmin=zlo, zmax=zhi, height=720,
        source_note="Source: Statistics Canada, 2021 Census (Table 98-10-0058-01); 2020 income.")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Census-tract income map not available — run `python -m pipeline.build_census_geo`.")
```

[← Back to Income & Inequality](index.qmd)
